name: Momotaro
description: Momotaro, the Peach Boy, is a beloved hero in Japanese folklore. Born from a giant peach found floating down a river by an elderly, childless couple, Momotaro quickly grew into a strong and courageous young man. Sensing a great evil plaguing the land in the form of Oni (demons) terrorizing a remote island, he sets out on a quest to defeat them. Along his journey, he befriends a talking dog, monkey, and pheasant, who pledge their loyalty and assist him in his battles. Momotaro's strength, bravery, and the unwavering loyalty of his companions allow him to successfully subdue the Oni, retrieve their stolen treasures, and bring peace back to the land. He returns home a celebrated hero, providing for the elderly couple who raised him and becoming a symbol of courage, filial piety, and the triumph of good over evil in Japanese culture. Momotaro’s story is often told to children, teaching them important values and reinforcing the importance of bravery, loyalty, and helping those in need. He represents the quintessential Japanese hero, embodying the ideals of strength, compassion, and unwavering determination.
subtitle: The Peach Boy, Oni Conqueror
achievements: Returned Stolen Treasure to the People
Became a Symbol of Courage and Filial Piety

keyMyths.title: The Discovery of Momotaro
keyMyths.description: An elderly, childless couple discovers a giant peach floating down a river. Upon opening it, they find a healthy baby boy inside, whom they name Momotaro.

keyMyths.title: Momotaro's Quest
keyMyths.description: Momotaro sets out to defeat the Oni on Onigashima, receiving Kibi Dango from his adopted parents for sustenance and attracting animal companions along the way.

keyMyths.title: The Defeat of the Oni
keyMyths.description: Momotaro and his companions successfully defeat the Oni, reclaiming stolen treasures and restoring peace to the land before returning home a hero.

sources.author: Yei Theodora Ozaki
sources.description: A collection of classic Japanese folktales, including the story of Momotaro.
sources.text: A collection of classic Japanese folktales, including the story of Momotaro.
